input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the vehicle on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the vehicle on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule V.1.4.2 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.1.4.2
            TITLE: the distance to the ground below the lower side impact structure ( f.6.4.5, f.7.5.1 ) at its

            CONTENT:
            rule V.1.4.2: The distance to the ground below the Lower Side Impact Structure ( F.6.4.5, F.7.5.1 ) at its
V.1.4.2 The distance to the ground below the Lower Side Impact Structure ( F.6.4.5, F.7.5.1 ) at its 
lowest point should be 75 mm or less

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, view outline, numerical value

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule V.1.4.2 requires that the distance to the ground below the Lower Side Impact Structure at its lowest point must be 75 mm or less. The provided engineering drawing does not explicitly show a dimension for the minimum ground clearance under the side impact structure. Following the instruction to assume compliance if a dimension is not explicitly shown, the design is deemed compliant with this rule.
Answer: Yes
